checks.finding: Every REQ-001..REQ-009 states concrete, measurable success conditions (exact schema fields/keys, byte-identical golden outputs, specific selector outcomes, explicit null/non-null field-population rules) and each is traced to at least one AC (e.g. REQ-001 -> AC-001..005; REQ-008 -> AC-047/048), so a downstream verifier can validate each requirement without further clarification.

requirements.md's Goals list (REQ-001..009) and acceptance-tests.md's table both carry explicit '(REQ-XXX)' tags on every AC-001..AC-050 row; every REQ number appears at least once as an AC's parenthetical requirement tag (verified REQ-001 through REQ-009 each covered).

AC-001..AC-050 describe externally checkable artifacts and behaviors (JSON schema field presence/values, byte-identical fixture comparisons, specific CLI-flag-driven selection outcomes, file write/no-write assertions, grep-based document presence checks) rather than vague intent statements.

requirements.md's Non-goals section explicitly excludes model-generation refresh, rewriting the codex CLI's own argument handling, the broader risk-adaptive-release-gating epic, making matrix the Phase-1 default, building an automated CI ordering gate for #155, direct edits to protected gate files, edits to the four named enforcement-chain test files, and tasks.md/traceability.md authoring.

A Security Boundaries table (B1-B4) covers input validation, protected-file write boundaries, CLI-argument-injection prevention, and applied-vs-null field integrity; a Roles and Permissions section states the human-copy approval boundary for protected reviewer files and T-007's prerequisite-gate approval; migration/compatibility constraints are explicit (v1 file byte-identical/frozen, AC-004/AC-006/AC-007/AC-025 backward-compatibility locks).

The Risks section lists Critical/High/Medium/Low items (e.g. protected-file mis-write, stale golden baseline, premature T-007 landing before A3) and each cites a specific Mitigation tied to an AC/self-check (AC-019's write-target self-check, AC-007's mutation-based negative self-check, AC-045/046's prerequisite-gate proof), so every high-risk claim has a planned validation surface.
